domain_name: Gig/platform labour precarity and worker agency

background_concepts.why_needed: These ethnographic studies of ride-hailing and delivery workers assume the reader understands algorithmic management—how platforms delegate task allocation, monitoring, and discipline to algorithms rather than human managers—without defining it.

suggested_resources.title: Gig Work, Algorithmic Technologies, and the Uncertain Future of Work (open chapter)

background_concepts.why_needed: The 'two-fold gamification' study analyzes how delivery-platform incentive design manipulates worker behavior, but assumes familiarity with game-design elements (points, badges, leaderboards, extrinsic vs. intrinsic motivation) as applied to non-game contexts.

suggested_resources.title: What is Gamification? — Interaction Design Foundation
Gamification and Game-Based Learning — University of Waterloo CTE

background_concepts.why_needed: The 'freedom-based inclusion' framing in the gendered-inequality paper draws directly on Amartya Sen's capability approach (functionings, agency, substantive freedoms) without explaining this theoretical foundation.

suggested_resources.title: Sen's Capability Approach — Internet Encyclopedia of Philosophy

domain_name: Algorithmic fairness interventions for platform workers

background_concepts.concept: Algorithmic management / work allocation on platforms
background_concepts.why_needed: These CS system-design papers propose fair-wage scheduling and dynamic safety checklists as interventions layered onto platform work-allocation systems; they assume the reader knows how baseline algorithmic task/wage allocation on gig platforms functions.

suggested_resources.title: The Algorithmic-Human Manager: work allocation and wage determination (open primer sections)

domain_name: MGNREGA / public employment guarantee as climate and welfare infrastructure

background_concepts.why_needed: All these papers presuppose knowledge of MGNREGA's institutional mechanics—100 days of guaranteed wage work, demand-driven design, Gram Panchayat implementation, and the water/soil/afforestation asset works that generate climate co-benefits.

suggested_resources.title: MGNREGS — Drishti IAS government schemes guide

background_concepts.why_needed: The carbon co-benefit papers quantify carbon sequestered by MGNREGA's land/water/afforestation works but assume the reader understands the physical process by which atmospheric CO2 is transferred into soil organic carbon and biomass.

suggested_resources.title: Soil Carbon Sequestration—Fundamentals — Ohio State Ohioline
Soil Carbon Sequestration — FAO Soils Portal

domain_name: Welfare allocation equity and datafication of marginalized workers

background_concepts.concept: Caste system and Scheduled Castes/Scheduled Tribes
background_concepts.why_needed: The caste-inequity analyses assume the reader understands India's caste hierarchy, the constitutional Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe categories, and reservation policy that structure how welfare discrimination is measured.

suggested_resources.title: An introduction to the basic elements of the caste system of India — Frontiers

background_concepts.why_needed: Studies of datafied welfare allocation and 'datafication encounters' assume knowledge of India's Aadhaar biometric ID and DBT infrastructure through which benefits are digitally targeted and can encode exclusion of marginalized workers.

suggested_resources.title: Aadhaar for Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) — UIDAI (Government of India)
Direct Benefit Transfer & the JAM Trinity explained

domain_name: Digital traces for labour market and migration measurement

background_concepts.why_needed: The Rwanda migration inference and Togo phone-sharing papers derive mobility and labour proxies from CDRs, assuming the reader knows what these mobile-operator records contain (caller/callee, time, duration, cell-tower location) and how tower data approximates location.

suggested_resources.title: Making the Most of Mobility Data (CDRs): A Guide for Policymakers — MIT GOV/LAB
How Analysis of Call Detail Records Provides Information for Development — ALNAP

domain_name: Digital platforms for social protection and inclusive employment access

background_concepts.concept: Hijra / third-gender communities in South Asia
background_concepts.why_needed: The Hijra employment-pathway paper assumes familiarity with hijra as a recognized third-gender community in South Asia and their historically marginalized socio-economic position, which shapes the inclusion intervention.

background_concepts.concept: Direct Benefit Transfer / Aadhaar-linked social protection
background_concepts.why_needed: Papers on connecting marginalized groups to social benefits assume the reader knows India's Aadhaar-linked DBT welfare-delivery infrastructure that civil-society intermediaries help populations navigate.

suggested_resources.title: Aadhaar for Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) — UIDAI (Government of India)

domain_name: Migrant and refugee worker safety and ICT-mediated support
